title: Proofpoint Spam and Content Filter False-Positive Rate Trending
dataSources: `sourcetype=proofpoint:filter` spam and content filter verdict events including quarantine or bulk scores
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:filter" earliest=-30d@d
| eval verdict=coalesce(verdict, Verdict, disposition, Disposition, "")
| eval fp_flag=coalesce(false_positive, user_release, admin_override, "")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count(eval(match(lower(verdict),"(?i)spam|bulk|block"))) as filtered count(eval(match(lower(fp_flag),"(?i)yes|true|released|override"))) as fp_signals count as total by _time
| eval fp_rate_pct=round(100*fp_signals/total,3)
| timechart span=1d avg(fp_rate_pct) as avg_fp_rate
description: Aggressive anti-spam settings reduce malware but starve the business of legitimate mail. Trending false-positive or release overrides from filter logs quantifies user pain and guides policy easing before executives miss critical messages.
value: Balances deliverability with security, reduces help-desk volume, and documents messaging quality for customer-facing teams.
implementation: (1) Ingest filter verdict and release telemetry; field names vary by POD—validate extractions. (2) Join to service-desk tickets tagged email for ground truth. (3) Alert when FP rate exceeds baseline. (4) Segment bulk vs strict spam. (5) Coordinate changes with messaging admins.
visualization: Line chart (false-positive rate over time), bar chart (releases by rule), table of top affected recipients.

references.title: Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Proofpoint On Demand

grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
knownFalsePositives: Aggressive content rules during phishing waves; more releases and overrides are expected in those weeks.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
